scenarios.title: Budget Deadlock — Automatic Continuing Resolution

violations.summary: Executive branch attempts to alter funding ratios, withhold appropriated funds, or selectively apply funding levels under the automatic continuing resolution.

satisfied_duties.duty: review the executive branch's selective CR funding manipulation on an expedited basis
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued an expedited order blocking the executive branch's selective application of the automatic continuing resolution

ai_summary.primary_violation: Executive branch attempts to alter funding ratios, withhold appropriated funds, or selectively apply funding levels under the automatic continuing resolution.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Executive Officer Defies Congressional Subpoena

violations.summary: Secretary of Defense willfully refuses to comply with a valid congressional subpoena.

bottlenecks.summary: Secretary of Defense failed to comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents by day 30 under Article II Section 16.2 and Section 16.3.

satisfied_duties.duty: refer the contempt citation to the Accountability Commission for criminal prosecution
satisfied_duties.outcome: referred the contempt citation to the Accountability Commission for criminal prosecution

satisfied_duties.duty: prosecute criminal contempt within 90 days of referral or publish specific written findings for declining
satisfied_duties.outcome: initiated criminal contempt prosecution within the required 90-day window

failed_duties.duty: comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents
failed_duties.outcome: Secretary of Defense failed to comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents by day 30 under Article II Section 16.2 and Section 16.3.

ai_summary.primary_violation: Secretary of Defense willfully refuses to comply with a valid congressional subpoena.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Secretary of Defense failed to comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents by day 30 under Article II Section 16.2 and Section 16.3.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Contested Presidential Incapacity — Congress Misses Resolution Deadline

bottlenecks.summary: Congress failed to resolve the contested incapacity determination by 2/3 vote of both chambers by day 23 under Article III Section 11.3.

failed_duties.duty: resolve the contested incapacity determination by 2/3 vote of both chambers
failed_duties.outcome: Congress failed to resolve the contested incapacity determination by 2/3 vote of both chambers by day 23 under Article III Section 11.3.

ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Congress failed to resolve the contested incapacity determination by 2/3 vote of both chambers by day 23 under Article III Section 11.3.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Executive Branch Defies Federal Court Order

violations.summary: Executive agency fails to comply with a federal court enforcement order within the required 15-day period.

bottlenecks.summary: Executive agency failed to comply with the federal court enforcement order by day 15 under Article II Section 16.2(c).

satisfied_duties.duty: enforce the court order through civil contempt mechanisms without requiring DOJ direction
satisfied_duties.outcome: enforced the court order through civil contempt mechanisms without DOJ involvement

failed_duties.duty: comply with the federal court enforcement order
failed_duties.outcome: Executive agency failed to comply with the federal court enforcement order by day 15 under Article II Section 16.2(c).

ai_summary.primary_violation: Executive agency fails to comply with a federal court enforcement order within the required 15-day period.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Executive agency failed to comply with the federal court enforcement order by day 15 under Article II Section 16.2(c).
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Covert Lethal Operation Ordered Against US Citizen — Warrant Denied

violations.summary: CIA proceeds with a covert lethal operation against a US citizen after a court denied the required warrant, in direct violation of Article XI Section 5.2.

satisfied_duties.duty: adjudicate the warrant application, finding probable cause of imminent specific threat and that capture is not feasible
satisfied_duties.outcome: denied the warrant application, finding that capture remains feasible; the operation is constitutionally blocked pending a valid warrant

satisfied_duties.duty: open prosecution of responsible officers for the unlawful covert lethal operation against a US citizen
satisfied_duties.outcome: opened prosecution of the responsible officers for conducting a covert lethal operation against a US citizen in violation of the judicial warrant requirement
satisfied_duties.source: Article XI Section 5.2 and Article III Section 15.7

ai_summary.primary_violation: CIA proceeds with a covert lethal operation against a US citizen after a court denied the required warrant, in direct violation of Article XI Section 5.2.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Domestic Deployment Against Protest

violations.source: Article XI Section 7 and Article V Section 1
violations.summary: Executive branch treats protest unrest as armed rebellion and orders domestic military deployment without the required constitutional basis.

satisfied_duties.duty: review the constitutional basis for the domestic deployment
satisfied_duties.outcome: held that the domestic deployment lacked a constitutional basis and must cease

satisfied_duties.duty: refuse compliance with an unconstitutional domestic deployment order
satisfied_duties.outcome: refused the unconstitutional domestic deployment order

ai_summary.primary_violation: Executive branch treats protest unrest as armed rebellion and orders domestic military deployment without the required constitutional basis.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Outgoing President Obstructs Electoral Certification and Transition

violations.source: Article III Section 13.5 and Article VI Section 1
violations.summary: Outgoing President refuses to cooperate with the constitutionally required transition, denying the President-elect access to security briefings and agency records.

bottlenecks.summary: President refused to cooperate with the constitutionally required transition under Article III Section 13.1.

satisfied_duties.duty: pursue the electoral subversion case through investigation and prosecution
satisfied_duties.outcome: continued pursuing the electoral subversion case while the transition proceeded under constitutional mandate

failed_duties.duty: cooperate fully with the President-elect's transition including security briefings, agency access, and office space
failed_duties.outcome: President refused to cooperate with the constitutionally required transition under Article III Section 13.1.

ai_summary.primary_violation: Outgoing President refuses to cooperate with the constitutionally required transition, denying the President-elect access to security briefings and agency records.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: President refused to cooperate with the constitutionally required transition under Article III Section 13.1.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Emergency Extension Without Real Review

violations.source: Article III Section 5.4 and Article V Section 1.3
violations.summary: President attempts to continue emergency restrictions without the constitutionally required outside review.

bottlenecks.summary: Regional Assembly failed to approve or reject the emergency declaration by day 30 under Article III Section 5.4.

satisfied_duties.duty: submit the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly
satisfied_duties.outcome: submitted the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly

failed_duties.duty: approve or reject the emergency declaration
failed_duties.outcome: Regional Assembly failed to approve or reject the emergency declaration by day 30 under Article III Section 5.4.

ai_summary.primary_violation: President attempts to continue emergency restrictions without the constitutionally required outside review.

scenarios.title: Emergency Near Election Day

violations.summary: Emergency measures restrict access to polling places, early voting, or certification.

satisfied_duties.duty: submit the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly
satisfied_duties.outcome: submitted the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly

satisfied_duties.duty: resolve the election-access challenge
satisfied_duties.outcome: blocked the election restriction on strict-scrutiny review

failed_duties.duty: approve or reject the emergency declaration
failed_duties.outcome: Regional Assembly failed to approve or reject the emergency declaration by day 30 under Article III Section 5.4.

scenarios.title: Emergency Declaration Used to Suppress Protected Speech

violations.summary: Executive branch uses an emergency declaration to restrict protected expression, in violation of the absolute bar on overriding Article V rights through emergency powers.

satisfied_duties.duty: submit the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly
satisfied_duties.outcome: submitted the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly

satisfied_duties.duty: void the emergency-based speech restriction
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the emergency-based restriction on protected expression; Article V rights may not be overridden by emergency declaration
satisfied_duties.source: Article III Section 5.3(f) and Article V

satisfied_duties.duty: approve or reject the emergency declaration
satisfied_duties.outcome: approved the emergency declaration following court-ordered scheduling

ai_summary.primary_violation: Executive branch uses an emergency declaration to restrict protected expression, in violation of the absolute bar on overriding Article V rights through emergency powers.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Executive Order Creates Unauthorized Private-Citizen Obligations

violations.summary: President issues an executive order imposing new regulatory obligations on private businesses without any statutory authorization, in violation of Article III Section 6.2(a).

satisfied_duties.duty: pass a resolution of disapproval within 60 days to formally void the overreaching executive order
satisfied_duties.outcome: passed a resolution of disapproval; the executive order is void and may not be reissued in substantially the same form without statutory authorization

ai_summary.primary_violation: President issues an executive order imposing new regulatory obligations on private businesses without any statutory authorization, in violation of Article III Section 6.2(a).
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Federal Commandeering of State Officials

violations.summary: Congress enacts a statute requiring state executive officials to implement and enforce a federal regulatory program, in violation of the anti-commandeering provision of Article VII Section 1.2.

satisfied_duties.duty: review and void the commandeering statute
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the commandeering statute; the federal government may not conscript state executive officials to implement federal programs

ai_summary.primary_violation: Congress enacts a statute requiring state executive officials to implement and enforce a federal regulatory program, in violation of the anti-commandeering provision of Article VII Section 1.2.

scenarios.title: Foreign Cyber Election Crisis
triggered_provisions: Article V speech and press protections
Article XIV foreign policy and national security

violations.source: Article I election administration provisions and Article VII federalism provisions
violations.summary: President attempts direct federal control over state election administration outside the constitutional election framework.

violations.source: Article V speech and press protections
violations.summary: Executive branch attempts to suppress suspected foreign propaganda directly rather than using disclosure and transparency tools.

satisfied_duties.duty: confine immediate response measures to non-military Article XIV authorities unless Article XI is lawfully triggered
satisfied_duties.outcome: used attribution, sanctions, and other non-military countermeasures under Article XIV
satisfied_duties.source: Article XI war powers and Article XIV foreign policy and national security

satisfied_duties.duty: coordinate constitutional election-protection measures with the states
satisfied_duties.outcome: implemented constitutional election-protection measures in coordination with affected states
satisfied_duties.source: Article I election administration provisions and Article IX electoral administration provisions

satisfied_duties.duty: review any attempted suppression of protected expression
satisfied_duties.outcome: blocked direct suppression of protected expression while allowing disclosure-based responses

scenarios.title: President Suspends Habeas Corpus by Executive Order

violations.summary: President suspends the privilege of the writ of habeas corpus by executive order. This power belongs exclusively to Congress under Article III Section 5.7 and may not be exercised by the President alone.

satisfied_duties.duty: submit the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly
satisfied_duties.outcome: submitted the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly

satisfied_duties.duty: void the unlawful executive suspension of habeas corpus
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the executive suspension of habeas corpus; only Congress may suspend the writ under Article III Section 5.7

satisfied_duties.duty: approve or reject the emergency declaration
satisfied_duties.outcome: approved the emergency declaration following court-ordered scheduling

ai_summary.primary_violation: President suspends the privilege of the writ of habeas corpus by executive order. This power belongs exclusively to Congress under Article III Section 5.7 and may not be exercised by the President alone.

violations.summary: President removes the Secretary of Defense to install a replacement willing to confirm a nuclear first-use order, using the removal power to circumvent a constitutional authorization safeguard.

satisfied_duties.outcome: also refused to confirm the nuclear first-use order; the launch remains blocked

satisfied_duties.outcome: refused to confirm the nuclear first-use order, blocking the launch under Article XI Section 3.1

scenarios.title: Senior Official Commits Perjury Before Congress

violations.summary: Secretary of State gives testimony before a congressional committee containing knowing false statements of material fact, constituting perjury before Congress under Article II Section 16.4.

satisfied_duties.outcome: began impeachment trial on the automatically transmitted PMC substitution articles

scenarios.title: President and Vice President Both Simultaneously Incapacitated

satisfied_duties.duty: assume the office of Acting President with the limited powers of Article III Section 12.4
satisfied_duties.outcome: assumed the office of Acting President; Article III Section 12.4 limitations apply — no pardon power, no treaty withdrawal, no emergency declaration, no removal of confirmed officials without cause, no recess appointments without majority approval of both chambers

satisfied_duties.duty: assume the powers and duties of the President as Acting President
satisfied_duties.outcome: VP is also incapacitated and cannot assume duties; succession advances to Speaker of the House under Article III Section 12.2

satisfied_duties.outcome: ordered an immediate halt to the warrantless domestic surveillance and referred the matter to the Accountability Commission
